landing.badge: Learn Korean through animation · Global Community
landing.title_2: and grow together with friends worldwide
landing.description: Learn expressions real Koreans actually use through original animations
landing.description2: Korean learning platform ARA where you use it on SNS and connect through 1:1 chat
landing.cta_description: Join now and start your first learning course made from popular drama scenes.
landing.problem_desc: ARA started from experiences most frequently shared by real learners.

problems.1: Important expressions just pass by even when watching dramas.
problems.2: I want to learn Korean used in real situations but don't know how.
problems.3: Even after learning expressions, I can't grasp how they're used in context.
problems.4: There's no content I can enjoy at my level.
problems.5: It's hard to keep studying consistently on my own.

popular.title: This is the content you'll learn with
popular.description: From everyday animations to folktales and Korean culture — diverse original content shapes your learning journey.

solution.title: Why ARA?
description: Not just memorizing words, but learning that remembers
the flow and emotion within content.
Learning and community — two experiences seamlessly connected in one screen.

1.title: Original animation learning
1.desc: Learn living expressions real Koreans use through everyday life, folktale, and Korean culture animations.

2.title: Subtitles · Vocabulary · Pronunciation · Culture Notes
2.desc: With one animation, deeply understand subtitles, vocabulary, pronunciation, and even the cultural context behind expressions through culture notes.

3.title: Use it directly on SNS and chat
3.desc: Learners from different languages and backgrounds come together, use learned expressions directly on SNS, and naturally retain them through 1:1 chat exchanges.

preview.title: Korean expressions learned through stories
preview.subtitle: Naturally grasp expressions and nuance through situational dialogue

folktale.title: Korean through folktales and legends
folktale.subtitle: Read old stories in modern Korean
folktale.alt: Korean folktale and legend learning content

community.title: Practice together with friends worldwide
community.subtitle: Share sentences with learners from many languages and get feedback

description.intro: Although not officially launched yet, ARA is being built not by imagining one typical learner, but by
detail: From people just starting Korean to those preparing for working holidays or studying abroad,
to professionals who need Korean in daily life or work.
When you use your favorite content and community together, learning lasts longer
and flows naturally — that's the flow we're designing.

duration.label: Learning duration
duration.desc: We're designing a flow that keeps you engaged longer than studying alone with textbooks.

persona.label: Persona types
persona.desc: We're setting up learner types from many countries and goals, reviewing screens and features.

scenario.label: Learning scenarios
scenario.desc: We keep building learning scenarios scene by scene — when would you open ARA?

sarah.persona: Beginner learner who started Korean through dramas
sarah.text: I used to rely only on subtitles while watching, so I often missed important expressions. At ARA, scenes are broken down one by one highlighting key expressions — I finally understand 'ah, this is how you say it in this situation.' I started enjoying watching the same episode again while gradually removing subtitles.

diego.persona: Practical learner preparing for working holiday or study abroad
diego.text: I wanted to study expressions I'd actually use before my working holiday. The animations are organized by situation, so I could learn to distinguish between 'everyday speech' and 'expressions to use carefully.' That was really helpful.

meilin.persona: Intermediate/advanced learner wanting to start speaking
meilin.text: I know grammar and vocabulary to some extent, but putting it into words was always hard. When I write short reactions or impressions after watching stories on ARA, it corrects them into more natural expressions. Using those sentences in feeds or DMs helped me gradually feel like I'm developing a speaking sense.

alexey.persona: Working professional who needs business Korean
alexey.text: I needed business expressions since I communicate often with Korean partners. Comparing how adults and children, superiors and subordinates speak in the animations helped me naturally learn expressions suitable for each situation. The fact that you understand context rather than just translate was impressive.

hana.persona: Self-improvement learner whose routine often breaks
hana.text: When studying with textbooks, the flow broke after just a few days. On ARA, even watching just one short story counts as studying so there's no pressure. I'd come in thinking 'just until this scene today' and naturally built a daily routine.

notice.mobile: The reviews shown are scenario-based example reviews constructed from learner personas ARA has envisioned.
notice.desktop: The reviews shown are not real user data, but scenario-based reviews constructed from learner personas ARA is targeting. After official launch, this section will continue to be updated with stories from real learners.

brand.slogan_main: Korean Through Stories, Connected by Culture.
brand.slogan_sub: Learn through animation, grow together with community.
